# Phase 10.0.5 Knowledge Canon Plan

Status: Planning-only. This plan does not create executable workflows, runtime authority, provider authority, infrastructure authority, deployment authority, autonomous governance, APIs, background jobs, automated doctrine enforcement, self-modifying canon systems, or execution authority.

## Objective

Define how civilization preserves, organizes, indexes, navigates, and maintains constitutional governance knowledge coherently across extreme long horizons.

## Scope

- Constitutional doctrine indexing philosophy.
- Governance knowledge canon doctrine.
- Institutional memory preservation architecture.
- Civilization doctrine classification standards.
- Constitutional traceability indexing doctrine.
- Governance knowledge lineage mapping.
- Doctrine discoverability continuity standards.
- Institutional memory navigation architecture.
- Constitutional canon survivability doctrine.
- Governance archive classification framework.

## Required Safeguards

- No runtime activation exists.
- No execution authorization exists.
- No deployment authorization exists.
- No provider onboarding exists.
- No orchestration activation exists.
- No infrastructure activation exists.
- No canon or indexing layer creates runtime authority.
- No doctrine indexing bypasses constitutional supremacy.
- No knowledge organization layer enables hidden execution escalation.
- No canon doctrine weakens auditability.

## Step Improvement Notes

- Correct build order: follows lineage, inheritance, and multi-generation continuity doctrine.
- Safe to include now: source-first canon, classification, traceability indexing, knowledge lineage mapping, discoverability, and navigation architecture.
- Future upgrade: manual canon inventory and archive manifest certification.
- Do not include: runtime enablement, providers, adapters, deployment paths, credentials, OAuth, APIs, fetch/axios, Prisma/Supabase changes, background jobs, autonomous governance, automated doctrine enforcement, self-modifying canon systems, or execution systems.
